concept.what: An agent is either a .py file or a .lispy file. They are the SAME agent. unRAPP converts one to the other. You never maintain both. You have whichever one you have, and unRAPP gives you the other on demand.
concept.no_new_format: There is NO .agent.json format. The agent IS the .py or the .lispy. Period. unRAPP is the operation that translates between them.
concept.the_name: unRAPP = unwrap a RAPP agent from one format to the other. RAPP = the agent in its current form. un = translate to the other side.

how_it_works.have_py_need_lispy: unRAPP reads basic_agent.py → extracts the class name, metadata, perform() logic → generates basic_agent.lispy with equivalent LisPy S-expression
how_it_works.have_lispy_need_py: unRAPP reads basic_agent.lispy → wraps in a BasicAgent Python class → generates basic_agent.py with LispyVM.run() calling the program
how_it_works.both_are_real: The .py is a real Python file. The .lispy is a real LisPy program. Neither is a "view" or "projection." Both execute natively on their runtime. unRAPP just translates.

why.no_twins: You dont maintain two files. You maintain ONE. When you need the other, unRAPP.
why.no_drift: There is no sync problem because only one file exists at a time.
why.adoption: AI-Agent-Templates users already have .py files. unRAPP gives them .lispy instantly. LisPy OS users have .lispy files. unRAPP gives them .py for Azure/Python.
why.the_bridge: unRAPP IS the bridge between the Python agent ecosystem and the LisPy VM ecosystem. Same agent. Same name. Same interface. Different runtime.

both_formats_have: The executable logic (perform() body in Python, S-expression in LisPy)
interface_contract.they_are_the_same: The perform() body in Python IS the LisPy program wrapped in vm.run(). The LisPy program IS the perform() body unwrapped.

unrapp.output: The OTHER format with the same name

rapp.description: Same as unrapp — the operation is symmetric. rapp and unrapp are the same thing. You have one side, you get the other.

file_rules.naming: EXACT same name. basic_agent.py ↔ basic_agent.lispy. Not lispy_basic_agent. Not basic_agent_lispy. SAME NAME.
file_rules.one_exists: Only ONE file needs to exist. The other is generated by unRAPP when needed.
file_rules.both_can_exist: If both exist (e.g., in a template library for convenience), they MUST be equivalent. Updating one means unRAPP-ing to regenerate the other.
file_rules.source_of_truth: Whichever file you edit IS the source of truth. unRAPP the other from it.
